summary: Iteration 167 frontend validation. Banner self-correction, sidebar normalization, ClientCRM navigation, AdviserDashboard viewClientPortal, and new AdviserScenarioWorkshop (4 sliders + chart + table) all PASS end-to-end. However the 3 Take Action wirings the main agent added in BookIntelligence / ClientIntelligenceFeed / DecisionEngine are UNREACHABLE — those components are imported but never mounted by any route in App.js. The paths mentioned in the review request (/book-intelligence, /intelligence, /decision-center) currently resolve to different components (redirect to /daily-briefing, IntelligenceEngine, DecisionCenter respectively), so the newly-added bookintel-take-action-*, intel-take-action-*, intel-schedule-*, decision-take-action-*, decision-learn-* testids never render in the live app and cannot be validated. All other regression checks (SimpleClientPortal on /client-portal, FloatingActionRail on 6 pages) pass.

integration_issues.issue: /book-intelligence is declared as <Navigate to='/daily-briefing' replace /> in App.js line 1194, so the BookIntelligence page with data-testid='bookintel-take-action-*' is unreachable. Take Action wiring added in BookIntelligence.jsx line ~246 cannot be used.

/intelligence route (App.js line 1167) is bound to IntelligenceEngine, not ClientIntelligenceFeed. The data-testid='intel-take-action-*' and 'intel-schedule-*' buttons live only in ClientIntelligenceFeed.jsx (no route references it anywhere). Buttons are not clickable because the component is never mounted.

/decision-center (App.js line 1148) is bound to DecisionCenter (scenario controls page), not DecisionEngine. The data-testid='decision-take-action-*' and 'decision-learn-*' buttons in DecisionEngine.jsx are unreachable.

action_items: Decide routing strategy for the 3 new Take Action wirings. Options: (a) rebind /book-intelligence → BookIntelligence, /intelligence → ClientIntelligenceFeed, /decision-center → DecisionEngine; (b) keep current routes and move the Take Action wiring into IntelligenceEngine / DecisionCenter / DailyBriefing instead; (c) add new alias routes like /book-intelligence-feed, /client-intelligence, /decision-engine. Once the components are actually rendered, re-test the 5 testid groups above.

(Nice-to-have) AdviserScenarioWorkshop baseline for Williams client_4 shows 97% confidence, spec expected ~92%. Thompson baseline = 100% (spec expected 99% ceiling — close). Not blocking but worth a quick param check if precise numbers matter for the narrative.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js imports DecisionEngine (line 124), ClientIntelligenceFeed (line 151), BookIntelligence (line 197) but never references them in a <Route> — tree-shaken-bundles aside, this is dead code and should either be routed or removed. Currently no URL surfaces the Take Action buttons the main agent added in those files.

AdviserScenarioWorkshop.jsx uses 300 Monte Carlo sims client-side on every slider tick (onValueChange). On low-end devices this may feel laggy. Consider debouncing the setState or reducing SIMS to 150.

AdviserScenarioWorkshop confidence can hit 100% (no cap). The narrative mentions a '99% ceiling' — no ceiling is enforced in code; Thompson naturally simulates at 100%. If a cap is required, enforce it in simulateProjection.
navigateToClient helper is clean and correct — single source of truth for slug resolution + localStorage write + navigate. Good pattern.

success_rate.frontend: ~70% — 5 of 8 feature groups fully pass; 3 Take Action routes unreachable

seed_data_creation: localStorage: app_mode='adviser', wealth_command_compliance_v5='permanent'. Also tested with STALE selected_client={id:'client_4',name:'Thompson Family',aum:1200000} to validate banner self-correction.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Banner self-correction + sidebar normalization VERIFIED: stale localStorage with id=client_4+name='Thompson Family'+aum=1200000 is overridden to 'Emma & David Williams' / '$4.0M' from CLIENT_DATA on both the top banner and the DesktopSidebar. ClientCRM first card click → /dashboard VERIFIED. AdviserScenarioWorkshop on /dashboard Retirement tab fully works: 4 sliders (budget/retire-age/contributions/volatility), live chart (ComposedChart with P10/P50/P90), comparison table with Current Plan vs Your Scenario rows, ws-summary badge showing confidence delta (e.g. '-31% vs current' when lowering retirement age). Thompson initial = 100%, Williams initial = 97%. FloatingActionRail confirmed on /daily-briefing, /meeting-prep, /adviser-hub, /tax-centre, /next-best-actions, /decision-center. UNRESOLVED: routes /book-intelligence, /intelligence, /decision-center do NOT render the components containing the new Take Action testids. Main agent must decide whether to rewire routes or move Take Action wiring elsewhere.
rca of the issue: App.js currently has: line 1148 '/decision-center' → <DecisionCenter /> (NOT DecisionEngine); line 1167 '/intelligence' → <IntelligenceEngine /> (NOT ClientIntelligenceFeed); line 1194 '/book-intelligence' → <Navigate to='/daily-briefing' replace />. The three components DecisionEngine, ClientIntelligenceFeed, BookIntelligence are imported with lazyRetry but have zero <Route> references in App.js (grep confirmed). The review request assumed these URLs render the newly-wired components, but the actual route bindings have drifted. Reproduction: open /book-intelligence in browser → lands on /daily-briefing. Open /intelligence → shows 'Intelligence Engine' page with 5 tabs, no 'intel-take-action-*' testids exist in the DOM. Open /decision-center → shows 'Decision Center' scenario controls page, no 'decision-take-action-*' testids exist. Mitigation: either wire Routes for the three components, or move the Take Action handlers into the currently-routed IntelligenceEngine / DecisionCenter / DailyBriefing pages so the wiring is reachable.
